sn36.7:1.1: I have heard that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Vesālī at the Gabled Hall in the Great Forest.
sn36.7:1.2: Then, emerging from his seclusion in the evening, he went to the sick ward. On arrival he sat down on a seat made ready.
sn36.7:1.3: Having sat down, he addressed the monks:
sn36.7:2.1: “A monk should approach the time of death mindful & alert.
sn36.7:2.2: This is our instruction to you all.
sn36.7:3.2: There is the case where a monk remains focused on the body in & of itself—ardent, alert, & mindful—putting aside greed & distress with reference to the world.
sn36.7:3.3: He remains focused on feelings in & of themselves…
sn36.7:3.5: mental qualities in & of themselves—ardent, alert, & mindful—putting aside greed & distress with reference to the world.
sn36.7:3.6: This is how a monk is mindful.
sn36.7:4.2: When going forward & returning, he makes himself fully alert; when looking toward & looking away… when bending & extending his limbs… when carrying his outer cloak, his upper robe, & his bowl… when eating, drinking, chewing, & savoring… when urinating & defecating… when walking, standing, sitting, falling asleep, waking up, talking, & remaining silent, he makes himself fully alert.
sn36.7:4.3: This is how a monk is alert.
sn36.7:4.4: “So a monk should approach the time of death mindful & alert.
sn36.7:4.5: This is our instruction to you all.
sn36.7:5.1: “As a monk is dwelling thus mindful & alert—heedful, ardent, & resolute—a feeling of pleasure arises in him. He discerns that
sn36.7:5.2: ‘A feeling of pleasure has arisen in me.
sn36.7:5.3: It is dependent on a requisite condition, not independent.
sn36.7:5.6: Now, this body is inconstant, fabricated, dependently co-arisen.
sn36.7:5.7: Being dependent on a body that is inconstant, fabricated, & dependently co-arisen, how can this feeling of pleasure that has arisen be constant?’
sn36.7:5.8: He remains focused on inconstancy with regard to the body & to the feeling of pleasure. He remains focused on dissolution… dispassion… cessation… relinquishment with regard to the body & to the feeling of pleasure.
sn36.7:5.9: As he remains focused on inconstancy… dissolution… dispassion… cessation… relinquishment with regard to the body & to the feeling of pleasure, he abandons any passion-obsession for the body & the feeling of pleasure.
sn36.7:6.1: “As he is dwelling thus mindful & alert—heedful, ardent, & resolute—a feeling of pain arises in him.
sn36.7:6.3: ‘A feeling of pain has arisen in me.
sn36.7:6.4: It is dependent on a requisite condition, not independent.
sn36.7:6.7: Now, this body is inconstant, fabricated, dependently co-arisen.
sn36.7:6.8: Being dependent on a body that is inconstant, fabricated, & dependently co-arisen, how can this feeling of pain that has arisen be constant?’
sn36.7:6.9: He remains focused on inconstancy with regard to the body & to the feeling of pain. He remains focused on dissolution… dispassion… cessation… relinquishment with regard to the body & to the feeling of pain.
sn36.7:6.10: As he remains focused on inconstancy… dissolution… dispassion… cessation… relinquishment with regard to the body & to the feeling of pain, he abandons any resistance-obsession for the body & the feeling of pain.
sn36.7:7.1: “As he is dwelling thus mindful & alert—heedful, ardent, & resolute—a feeling of neither-pleasure-nor-pain arises in him. He discerns that
sn36.7:7.2: ‘A feeling of neither-pleasure-nor-pain has arisen in me.
sn36.7:7.3: It is dependent on a requisite condition, not independent.
sn36.7:7.6: Now, this body is inconstant, fabricated, dependently co-arisen.
sn36.7:7.7: Being dependent on a body that is inconstant, fabricated, & dependently co-arisen, how can this feeling of neither-pleasure-nor-pain that has arisen be constant?’
sn36.7:7.8: He remains focused on inconstancy with regard to the body & to the feeling of neither-pleasure-nor-pain. He remains focused on dissolution… dispassion… cessation… relinquishment with regard to the body & to the feeling of neither-pleasure-nor-pain.
sn36.7:7.9: As he remains focused on inconstancy… dissolution… dispassion… cessation… relinquishment with regard to the body & to the feeling of neither-pleasure-nor-pain, he abandons any ignorance-obsession for the body & the feeling of neither-pleasure-nor-pain.
sn36.7:8.1: “Sensing a feeling of pleasure, he discerns that it is inconstant, not grasped at, not relished.
sn36.7:8.3: Sensing a feeling of neither-pleasure-nor-pain, he discerns that it is inconstant, not grasped at, not relished.
sn36.7:8.4: Sensing a feeling of pleasure, he senses it disjoined from it.
sn36.7:8.6: Sensing a feeling of neither-pleasure-nor-pain, he senses it disjoined from it.
sn36.7:8.7: When sensing a feeling limited to the body, he discerns that ‘I am sensing a feeling limited to the body.’ When sensing a feeling limited to life, he discerns that ‘I am sensing a feeling limited to life.’
sn36.7:8.8: He discerns that ‘With the break-up of the body, after the termination of life, all that is experienced, not being relished, will grow cold right here.’“
sn36.7:9.1: Just as an oil lamp burns in dependence on oil & wick; and from the termination of the oil & wick—
sn36.7:9.2: and from not being provided any other sustenance—it goes out unnourished;
sn36.7:9.3: in the same way, when sensing a feeling limited to the body, he discerns that ‘I am sensing a feeling limited to the body.’ When sensing a feeling limited to life, he discerns that ‘I am sensing a feeling limited to life.’
sn36.7:9.4: He discerns that ‘With the break-up of the body, after the termination of life, all that is sensed, not being relished, will grow cold right here.’”
